description: Distribution market on how many 25bp Fed rate cuts will occur in 2026. Six outcomes (0 through 5+). Modal outcome is 0 cuts at 37.4%, with the market pricing ~63% chance of at least one cut. This is a negRisk grouped market where all outcomes sum to 100%.
question: How many Fed rate cuts in 2026?

resolution_logic.summary: Resolves to the exact number of 25bp cuts made by the Fed in 2026 (including the December meeting). A 50bp cut counts as 2 cuts. Cuts between 1-24bp count as 1 cut. Emergency cuts outside scheduled FOMC meetings count. Individual outcome markets resolve early to No if the specified count becomes impossible.

triggers.description: Count of 25bp-equivalent rate cuts across all 2026 FOMC meetings
triggers.threshold: Exact match to outcome bucket (0, 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5+)

exclusions: Rate hikes do not reduce the cut count
A 50bp cut is counted as 2 cuts, not 1
resolution_logic.ambiguity_notes: The 1-24bp threshold is unusual — a hypothetical 10bp 'technical adjustment' would count as a full cut. The market stays open through Dec 31 to capture any emergency inter-meeting cuts. Early resolution only applies to individual buckets becoming impossible (e.g., if 2 cuts happen, the '0 cuts' and '1 cut' markets resolve No immediately).

catalyst_dates.event: FOMC meeting (June) — with updated dot plot and SEP
FOMC meeting (September) — with updated dot plot and SEP
FOMC meeting (December) — final 2026 meeting, with dot plot

cross_platform_link.structural_notes: Kalshi uses per-meeting binary decisions (hold/cut/hike at each FOMC), while Polymarket uses a year-end distribution (total cut count). Structurally incompatible — a Kalshi trader must piece together 6 individual meeting markets to reconstruct Polymarket's distribution. ClearMarket already tracks Kalshi's April and December FOMC holds (FOMC-APR2026-HOLD, FOMC-DEC2026-HOLD). The term structure across meeting dates creates natural arb opportunities when Kalshi per-meeting probabilities imply a different distribution than Polymarket's year-end prices.

related_markets.notes: Kalshi April FOMC hold market — one of 6 component decisions that determine the 2026 cut count
Kalshi December FOMC hold market — last meeting of the year, final determinant
Kalshi end-of-year rate level brackets — if rate is >3.50% (previously 49%), implies fewer cuts
Recession probability (37.5%) drives cut expectations — recession would likely force 3+ cuts
